content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA of length NNN.

There is an integer variable XXX and a score SSS. Initially, X=0X = 0X=0 and S=0S = 0S=0.

You play the following single-player game on the array AAA for the next NNN seconds:


In the ttt-th second (1≤t≤N1 \le t \le N1≤t≤N), you **may** choose any index iii such that 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N and t∣it \mid it∣i (i.e., iii is a multiple of ttt).
Then, update the score SSS as follows: S=S+∣X−Ai∣S = S + \lvert X - A_i \rvertS=S+∣X−Ai​∣.
Then, set X=AiX = A_iX=Ai​.
Alternatively, you can also choose to do nothing in the ttt-th second (both XXX and SSS remain unchanged).
Define f(A)f(A)f(A) to be the maximum possible final score SSS that can be achieved by playing the game optimally.

You are given an array AAA of length NNN. You need to handle QQQ updates to the array. Each update is of the form (i,x)(i, x)(i,x) and means: set Ai=xA_i = xAi​=x. Output the value of f(A)f(A)f(A) after each update.

**Important note on test generation (read carefully):** For all the official tests, the update index iii in each pair (i,x)(i, x)(i,x) is generated uniformly at random from {1,2,…,N}\{1, 2, \dots, N\}{1,2,…,N}, independently across updates. Note that only the indices are constrained this way, the new values xxx may be arbitrary within the given bounds.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ — the number of elements in the array and the number of updates to handle, respectively.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the initial array.
The next QQQ lines describe the updates. The kkk-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers iki_kik​ and xkx_kxk​, indicating the assignment Aik=xkA_{i_k} = x_kAik​​=xk​.




Output Format
After each update, output on a new line the current value of f(A)f(A)f(A).
constraints: Each iki_kik​ is generated uniformly randomly in the interval [1,N][1, N][1,N].
The sum of NNN across all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105
The sum of QQQ across all test cases won't exceed 5⋅1045 \cdot 10^45⋅104
